evidence.scientific_clarity: Stacked bars clearly show 4-class proportions summing to 1 across all 5 conditions; y-axis uses percent labels and spans 0–100%.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 and svg.fonttype=none set in rcParams; pdffonts confirms CID TrueType Arial embedded (no Type 3); tick/label sizes 8–9pt.
evidence.palette: Okabe-Ito 4-color subset (blue, orange, green, pink) used — fully colorblind-safe; no red/green directional misuse for categorical data.
evidence.layout_density: 6×4 in figure with tight_layout(pad=0.6), bar_width=0.55, y-range 0–1.0; no gaping whitespace or overlapping labels.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Legend placed top-right away from data; caption matches figure; no stray annotations; clean spine removal (top/right).
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts shows GOFYPY+ArialMT as CID TrueType embedded/subsetted; pdf.fonttype=42 honored; genuine vector PDF.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf, figure.png (300 dpi), figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption names the finding with specific percentages (~44% Class C in Cond2/Cond3; 37% Class A in Cond5), cites palette, and notes N=1 per condition.

overall_strengths: Exemplary technical rigor: Okabe-Ito palette, pdf.fonttype=42, embedded Arial, and a post-save font verification step all in one script.
Caption delivers a precise numerical finding tied directly to the visible data, meeting the requirements of a publication-ready figure.
overall_weaknesses: Font fallback chain still lists DejaVu Sans as third option; if Arial and Helvetica are absent the default would revert to DejaVu Sans, which is the very default to avoid.
No explicit data labels or error indicators inside the bars; for N=1 this is acceptable, but a reviewer might request at least visible segment boundaries.
